entry.title: SAP Road Map Explorer leverages the new Reference Capability Model
entry.content:
Navigating SAP’s broad innovation portfolio can be challenging. To better address the business context of the innovation, the SAP Road Map Explorer now relies on the SAP Reference Architecture Content enabling you to view innovations through a consistent, business-driven lens. The SAP Road Map Explorer uses capabilities to group and view innovations on the Kanban Board. These capabilities are organized and structured in the Reference Capability Model which has undergone a remodeling recently. Hence, in some cases, starting April 29, you will experience changes in titles or a different granularity in capabilities. Did you save roadmaps with “Line of Business” or “Business Capability” as filter criteria in the past? In this case, we recommend you download these saved roadmaps before April 29 and revisit your saved roadmaps in the Road Map Explorer after that date. You can then add Business Domains and/or Business Capabilities to your roadmaps and save them with a new title. For your reference, you find details on the mapping of legacy to new entries here.

 

What Is the Reference Capability Model?

The SAP Reference Capability Model is a standardized, enterprise-wide capability taxonomy built upon SAP’s Enterprise Architecture methodology. It provides a harmonized framework that maps business capabilities and processes to IT solutions, establishing a shared business language across SAP’s entire portfolio.

At its core, the model is composed of two interconnected parts:

  • SAP Reference Business Architecture (RBA) — describes the scope and undertaking of any enterprise in a business-centric, product-agnostic way. It defines what an organization needs to deliver to its customers through the concept of Business Capabilities.
  • SAP Reference Solution Architecture (RSA) — explores how SAP addresses a customer’s business challenge using its unique product portfolio, translating business needs into implementable solution architectures.

Together, these two components form a bridge between business and IT at the strategic level — enabling organizations to move systematically from identifying a business challenge to deploying the right SAP solution.

 

Why Was It Introduced?

SAP introduced the Reference Capability Model in response to a growing need for consistency, clarity, and alignment across its vast ecosystem of tools, teams, and customer touchpoints. Previously, the absence of a unified taxonomy created fragmentation across systems, making it difficult to deliver a coherent customer experience or maintain consistent solution positioning.

The transition to this new Capability Model reflects SAP’s broader Enterprise Architecture vision: to systematically guide customers from their business challenges to the most relevant SAP solutions.

 

Key Benefits

The adoption of the Reference Capability Model delivers measurable value across multiple dimensions:

  • Improved Operational Efficiency - A single, unified taxonomy eliminates redundancy and inconsistency across all relevant systems and tools.
  • Streamlined Customer Experience - Customers benefit from a coherent and consistent engagement across all SAP touchpoints.
  • Consistent Solution Positioning - Sales, presales, and delivery teams operate from the same structured framework, ensuring aligned messaging.
  • Full Transparency into SAP Software Adoption - Organizations gain end-to-end visibility into how SAP solutions are being utilized across their landscape.
  • A Shared Business Language - A common vocabulary aligns business and IT stakeholders, facilitating clearer communication and faster decision-making.

 

Conclusion

By using the new Capability Model, SAP Road Map Explorer builds on a robust, scalable foundation that supports both strategic planning and operational execution and empowers customers and internal teams alike to navigate the full SAP portfolio with confidence and clarity.

entry.title: Change log for reservation under lineitem
entry.content:
IN ME22N Tcode(PO Change) We have Environment tab we found header changes and item changes.
Same, we need to implement in the MB22 Tcode. There is an environment tab, but there is no scope to view the header and item changes. so we need to incorporate the header and item changes regarding reservations.In report level also like MB25 , There is no scope to view the change log regarding reservation.In table level(RESB) we found header changes.So need to incorporate the change log regarding reservation.For example, in reservation we have 4 line items, the first lineitem ''X'' user deleted and the next line item ''Y' user changed the quantity here We need to view the changes like Which user changed the data. (Either X Or Y).

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam instance will be about Event-driven integration using SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Join us for this event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Hyderabad, India.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one in the diagram below by the end of the CodeJam.

If you are interested in knowing what we will develop during the event, check out the Event-Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will go through during the event.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, June 23, 2026, from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Hyderabad, India (IST). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address:  Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited, Sattva Knowledge Park, Tower 1, 14th to 19th Floor Raidurg Village, Serilingampally Mandal Ranga Reddy District, Hyderabad, Telangana, 500081

Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/jdstx227U4YHzABb6

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Bosch Global Software Technologies Private Limited – THANK YOU!
  • The instructor is SAP Developer Advocate  @ajay_soreng

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees for this event.

In that context, if you indicate that you're attending and later realise you can't, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

entry.title: SAP Document AI – Quality Certificate processed successfully but Certificate Receipt remains in “Exp
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

 

I am working on the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition scenario
“AI-assisted processing of incoming supplier quality certificates with SAP Document AI”.

 

Current setup:
- AI Units are procured
- Business catalog QM – Incoming Quality Certificates (SAP_QM_BC_INCG_QLTY_CERT_PC) is assigned
- Scope item 7TC (Integration with SAP Document AI) is activated
- SAP Document AI tenant is accessible
- Certificate Receipt is created automatically at Goods Receipt with status = Expected

 

What I have done:
1. Posted Goods Receipt – Certificate Receipt created in Manage Certificate Receipts (F3233) with status Expected
2. Uploaded the supplier quality certificate PDF in SAP Document AI using:
   - Document Type: Custom
   - Schema: SAP_OCROnly_schema
3. Document AI processing completes successfully and shows status = Done

 

Issue:
Even after successful processing in SAP Document AI, the Certificate Receipt in SAP S/4HANA Cloud remains in “Expected” status and is not automatically updated to “Received”.
The document is not automatically attached to the Certificate Receipt.

 

Questions:
1. Is automatic update of Certificate Receipt (Expected → Received) the expected behavior in this scenario?
2. Is manual confirmation in Manage Certificate Receipts (F3233) the standard SAP-supported fallback if automatic matching does not occur?
3. Does the automatic update require uploading the document specifically via the app “Upload Quality Certificate Documents (F8858)” instead of uploading through the SAP Document AI portal?
4. If the “Upload Quality Certificate Documents (F8858)” app is used and it is not available in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library, how can access to this app be obtained in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
5.  Are there any additional configuration or prerequisites required to enable automatic linkage between SAP Document AI and Certificate Receipts?

 

Looking for confirmation from experts who have implemented or tested this scenario.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2605 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba Source to Contract Community, and welcome to our 2605 Key Innovations blog.
In this post, we’re sharing the main updates included in the Q2 2026 release for SAP Ariba source-to-contract solutions.

Here’s a closer look at what’s new this quarter.

SAP Ariba Contract Management

Line Item ID Display in Contract Workspaces (SCONTR-1563)

We have introduced a permanent Id field for line items within the Contract Line Items Document (CLID). Previously, the system-generated identifier was not visible in the UI, which made referencing specific lines difficult during amendments or when integrating with external systems.

 

Enhanced Supplier Risk Insights (SCONTR-1536)

For customers using SAP Ariba Supplier Risk alongside Contracts, workspaces linked to an engagement request now display two additional fields: "Engagement ID" and "Residual Risk Rating".

This provides direct visibility into the risk status of an engagement without requiring users to navigate away from the contract workspace.

 

SAP Ariba Sourcing

Allow Event Content to be set to Zero Weight (PSS-355)

A new Zero Weight toggle is available for event content such as questions, sections, or items. This is intended for content that requires a response or a grade (like contact information or general comments), but should not influence the supplier's final score.

Here is how it'll make a different:

  • Difference from "0" Weight: Unlike entering a "0" in the weight column (which prevents child content from having weight), the Zero Weight toggle allows you to still grade the item and maintain weights for any nested child content.

  • Grading: Members of the Team Graders group can still provide grades for these items, and these grades will appear on the scorecard without affecting the total.

 

Support for Dynamic Market Notices in ETendering (PSS-308)

For the United Kingdom Central Digital Platform (CDP), we now support dynamic market notices. These operate at the sourcing project level. While the framework tracker helps manage eligible suppliers, users can also manually add suppliers who are not part of the tracker via the Suppliers tab in the Dynamic Market Establishment notice.

 

Large Attachment Support (SOURCE-2797)

Users can now upload attachments up to 2 GB in size within both guided sourcing and the classic sourcing interface.

While this increases the capacity for technical specifications or large documents, please note that this does not currently support Smart imports, Bid imports, or Excel imports.

 

Expand and Collapse Nested Item Rows (SOURCE-2801)

To improve the handling of large events in guided sourcing, we have added expand and collapse icons for nested items on the Monitor and Award pages.

To improve performance, the system now loads the first 20 rows initially and loads further rows as the user scrolls.

 

Improved Navigation to the "Grade as team member" Page (SOURCE-2274)

We have simplified the steps required to access grading tasks:

  • A "Grade as team member" option is now available under the more options icon in the Review Team Grading Task.

  • A direct button has been added to the For You page.

  • The "Filter by" dropdown on the grading page now defaults to "Gradable" to help users find pending tasks faster.

 

These features can be adopted independently, depending on your current setup and processes.

Stay tuned for our additional upcoming blog posts and follow me to stay informed on upcoming feature releases across SAP Ariba. Once, these items are officially released on May 14th, the feature reference IDs (i.e. SC-22524) can help to find more detailed information on http://help.sap.com.

Thank you for being a part of the SAP Ariba community and we look forward to continuing our journey of innovation together!

Resources:

  • Feature related materials:
    • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
    • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
  • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
  • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
  • What’s New

entry.title: 🇩🇪 Hands-on with CAP CDS
entry.content:
This CodeJam gives you a thorough grounding in CAP CDS modelling.

In this hands-on workshop you'll become familiar and comfortable with the key features of CDS modelling, primarily focusing on the Conceptual Definition Language (CDL), the predominantly declarative domain-specific language (DSL) in the CDS family. You'll explore the different concepts that make CAP such a compelling framework for today's world of AI, where concision, correctness and the imperative to write less code is at the forefront of everyone's mind.

No prior experience is required, and this workshop is suitable for non-developers as well as developers. All you need is curiosity and a desire to learn!

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is on Fri 18 Sep 2026 in Weinheim, DE, kindly hosted by Syntax, thank you very much! Note that it takes place on the day before the SAP Inside Track Weinheim event which is on Sat 19 Sep 2026.

(For more info on this double-event, see the blog post Two days of SAP community in Weinheim: CodeJam and Inside Track, 18-19 September 2026).

Registration for this CodeJam must be done on the SAP CodeJam Weinheim - Hands-on with CAP CDS page which has been specially set up for this event.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

entry.title: Re: Updating property of a dimension via another property
entry.content:
Hi,

I have tried using Updatemember but the issue is you have to pass the variable in the API of type Planningmodelmember which should have id and properties and I want to pass only properties in that. Below is the code

var member=Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();
var rel=InputField_1.getValue();
ScriptVariable_1=({id:member,properties:{LE_FLAG:rel}});
PlanningModel_1.updateMembers("COSTOBJ",ScriptVariable_1);

var member should be L2 property and not member id

entry.title: Advancements in Cloud ALM and Configuration & Security Analysis application
entry.content:
As SAP landscapes grow and become more hybrid, maintaining a secure and compliant system configuration is no longer a one-time task - it’s an ongoing responsibility.  

The Configuration & Security Analysis (CSA) application in SAP Cloud ALM Operations is designed to support exactly this. This blog walks you through the new advancements in the SAP Cloud ALM and Configuration Analysis application. 

The goal is to make sure that all cloud services are operating and compliant to applicable audit standards and with an acceptable security posture. 

🌟What’s New? 

You can now: 

✔️ Validate your systems and services against  SAP security baseline - SAP Security Optimization Services 

✔️Display the compliance status for your favourite systems in the CSA Home screen. 

✔️Restrict access of users to specific services and systems. 

✔️Create notifications, alerts and tickets.  

For more information kindly visit : Configuration & Security Analysis 

🔗Stay connected 

Want to stay up to date on our services? Join our SAP Cloud ALM & Cross-Solution Topics community!

entry.title: The Advanced Success Plan in Detail – Getting More Value from Your SAP Cloud Investment
entry.content:
In the second session of the SAP Success Plans webcast series in April 2026, Jesper Waaben, Chief Product Owner for the Advanced Success Plans in the ERP space, took a closer look at what the Advanced Success Plan actually delivers. While the first webcast covered the big picture across all three plan tiers, this session focused on the specifics — the services available, the new Success Pack format, and what has genuinely changed compared to the predecessor, Preferred Success.

Almost half of the attendees described themselves as being in the early stages of their cloud transformation, which makes the timing of this new offering particularly relevant. Find below a summary and here the slides and the english webcast recording.

What stays, what's new 

The Advanced Success Plan builds on familiar ground plus enters new territory.

What stays is the proactive partnership model, the technical and functional support, and the release guidance that customers already know from Preferred Success. Anyone who has worked with Preferred Success will recognize many of the elements.

What's new is where it gets interesting.

The most tangible change is what SAP calls Hands-on Activation. Under Preferred Success, SAP experts were not allowed to touch customer systems and everything was strictly "hands off." That has changed now. Experts can now activate new functionalities directly in the system and guide initial, lightweight configuration steps. This naturally needs to be coordinated with the project team and, where applicable, the implementation partner — but it removes a long-standing limitation that many customers found frustrating.

A second major addition is AI-powered Guidance, integrated into SAP Cloud ALM. It analyses system data, particularly for SAP S/4HANA Private and Public Cloud environments and derives recommendations for process improvements, feature adoption, and system landscape decisions.

Beyond that, the Advanced Plan now covers end-to-end scenarios across the full SAP Business Suite, comes with improved SLAs (50% faster initial response times and accelerated corrective actions compared to the Foundational Plan), and incorporates elements that previously required the more expensive Preferred Success Expanded Edition — meaning customers effectively get more for less.

The five pillars of the Advanced Success Plan from SAP

The Advanced Plan extends the Foundational Success Plan through five areas.

Innovation and Adoption Guidance is about SAP experts working together with the Customer Success Manager to analyse specific functionalities and processes within a given solution — including cross-functional scenarios. The output is a set of recommendations on what to improve and which new features to activate.

Activation and Optimization Services is where those recommendations get implemented. SAP consultants go into the system, activate functionalities, and guide first configuration steps. Waaben made a point of emphasising that the experts deployed here are experienced SAP consultants from project work, not a pure support organization.

AI-powered Quality and Process Guidance uses the data collected in Cloud ALM to generate insights and recommendations. The prerequisite is that Cloud ALM is actively used — without it, this particular value stream doesn't fully materialize.

Orchestration of the Success Plan is something Waaben described as frequently underestimated. Customer Success Managers and Success Plan Managers ensure the right services are deployed at the right time. The goal is to move away from a scattergun approach toward targeted service delivery. The orchestrators can pull in the right SAP experts for any topic across any solution.

Enhanced Support SLAs deliver 50% faster initial response times and accelerated corrective actions — a relevant factor for anyone running business-critical systems.

Graphic: Advanced & Foundational Success Plan

Service Maps and Success Packs - making complexity manageable

For each SAP solution — whether S/4HANA Public, S/4HANA Private, Supply Chain, Ariba, SuccessFactors, CX, or Business Data Cloud, there is a dedicated Service Map showing which services are available, ranging from focused functional services to transformative formats like the Cloud Mindset Workshop.

Across all solutions, SAP offers what it calls Success Expert Services, including Value Management (establishing a baseline for tracking value over the solution lifecycle), Service Planning (workshop-based mapping of the customer situation to the right services), Adoption Checkpoints (reviewing the solution for untapped potential), and Innovation Checkpoints (evaluating new releases and AI capabilities for the specific customer context).

As the number of available services grows, so does complexity. SAP addresses this through Success Packs, thematic bundles of related services. Examples include Implementation Essentials, Finance Foundation, and Finance Expansion for S/4HANA Public; Clean Core, Business AI CoE, and Finance Optimization for S/4HANA Private; and dedicated packs for Supply Chain, Ariba, SuccessFactors, CX, and BTP. Customers don't have to book every service within a pack. The packs serve as orientation and shift the conversation from individual service tickets to business processes and outcomes.

Business AI — structured support across every stage

AI was a recurring theme throughout the session. Waaben showed how the three plan tiers work together when it comes to AI adoption. The Foundational Plan provides initial guidance in a self-service format. The Advanced Plan adds expert advisory on governance, compliance, and Clean Core for AI, workshops to identify AI use cases, activation of Joule, Agents, and intelligent apps, as well as AI Business Readiness Workshops to prepare the organization. The Max Plan then takes identified use cases into prototyping and custom development.

This matters because the webcast audience data confirmed that AI and cloud migration are top-of-mind topics. When asked about their transformation focus, 38.5% of respondents cited cloud migration, 30.8% Clean Core, and 7.7% AI and Business Data Cloud — all areas where the Advanced Plan delivers targeted support.

This graphic also illustrate the benefit of Success Plans. The CSM orchestrates important tasks from discovery to activation to reap the full benefit of AI. Otherwise there may be a break after discovery of AI use-cases. With a Success Plan in Plan like the Advanced Plan you will have a coordinated structured plan and execution against that plan. 

Market validation of the Advanced Success Plan

Two independent studies by IDC and Forrester were conducted with a focus on Preferred Success but are applicable to the new model. The confirmed three key findings.

  • First, access to SAP expertise is business-critical given the scale of investment in SAP systems.
  • Second, business users expect faster returns from IT investments, which increases pressure on IT departments.
  • Third, AI is fundamentally changing the software landscape and investment horizons, and customers are actively seeking guidance.

According to both analyses, customers with Success Plans reach their project goals faster and with greater confidence. The market response to the new model has been strong. In the first two months after launch, more than 200 customers purchased the Advanced Success Plan.

Practical recommendations

For organizations considering the Advanced Success Plan, a few things are worth keeping in mind. Start using Cloud ALM early, as the AI-powered guidance depends on it, and early adoption means stronger analytical insights down the line.
Use Success Packs as your entry point rather than picking individual services from the catalogue; bundles like "Clean Core" or "Finance Expansion" provide better orientation. Engage your Customer Success Manager actively to identify the right services at the right time — it's not about volume but about fit.
Coordinate Hands-on Activation with your project team and implementation partner to avoid friction. Consider starting with an AI Business Readiness Workshop to prepare your organisation before diving into specific use cases. And set up Value Management early so the benefits of the Success Plan become measurable and easier to communicate internally.

Bottom line

The Advanced Success Plan is a meaningful evolution from Preferred Success. The shift from "hands off" to hands-on activation addresses a long-standing customer need, while Success Packs bring structure to an increasingly broad service portfolio. The integrated AI support across all plan tiers positions the Success Plans as a practical companion for organisations navigating their SAP cloud journey — whether the focus is on migration, optimisation, or preparing for AI.

Further information on the Success Plans is available in the overview blog: SAP Success Pläne – Der Guide zum neuen SAP Support und Service Modell 2026. An on-site event is also planned for 25 June 2026 in Walldorf. 

FAQ - Questions and Answers from the discussion

What is the difference between the Advanced Success Plan and Preferred Success?
The Advanced Plan builds on Preferred Success but brings substantial extensions. Hands-on Activation allows SAP experts, for the first time, to activate functionalities directly in customer systems and guide lightweight configurations. On top of that, there is AI-powered guidance through Cloud ALM, improved SLAs, and broader coverage across the full Business Suite. Worth noting is that elements from the Preferred Success Expanded Edition, which used to be an even more expensive tier,  are now included in the Advanced Plan. In effect, customers get more for less.

Is there a maximum number of consulting days or tickets in the Advanced Plan?
No. The number of services in the Advanced Plan is not capped. Customers can book as many services as they need, as often as they need them.

What are Success Packs?
Success Packs are thematic bundles of related services — for example "Clean Core", "Finance Expansion", or "Demand Planning". They serve as a navigation aid and shift the conversation from individual service tickets to business topics and processes. Customers are not required to book all the services contained in a pack.

What role does the implementation partner play in the Success Plan model?
SAP does not position the Success Plans as competition to implementation partners. Hands-on Activation needs to be coordinated with the project team and, where applicable, the partner. SAP provides expertise on best practices, standards, and architecture — implementation work can stay with the partner.

Do I need to use Cloud ALM to benefit from the Advanced Plan?
For the full value of the AI-powered guidance, Cloud ALM is a prerequisite. The other services — Activation, Adoption Guidance, Expert Advisory — can be used independently of Cloud ALM.

Who are the experts delivering the Advanced services?
The experts are experienced SAP consultants from project work and seasoned Customer Success professionals who deliver services for the Advanced and, in part, the Max Plan. This is not a pure support organisation.

Which SAP solutions have Service Maps?
Service Maps are available for S/4HANA Public, S/4HANA Private, Supply Chain/IBP, Ariba, SuccessFactors, Customer Experience (Sales & Service Cloud, Commerce Cloud, Marketing Cloud), BTP, and Business Data Cloud.

How does the Advanced Plan support Business AI specifically?
The Advanced Plan includes expert advisory on AI governance and compliance, workshops to identify AI use cases, AI Business Readiness Workshops, and the activation of Joule, Agents, and intelligent apps. It supports you along every stage of your AI adoption journey. For taking identified use cases into prototyping, the Max Plan is required.

Is there any market evidence yet?
Yes. In the first two months after launch, more than 200 customers purchased the Advanced Success Plan. Independent analyses by IDC and Forrester confirm that customers with Success Plans reach their project goals faster and with greater confidence.

entry.content:

However, our requirement is to automatically link (or create and assign) this DIR document directly within the “Object Links” tab of the respective project.

entry.title: Impossible to copy/paste an object, from one page of a report to another, or recreate it
entry.content:
I have a report with 3 pages. From the 2nd page, I need to copy some of the scripting code lines and objects, to a 3rd recently created page. When finished, the 3rd page will replace the 2nd one.
I can copy/paste the code lines, but not all the objects (those referenced in the script). 
For only one of them (of type "calculated input control"), it's impossible for me to copy/paste this object, or even simply recreate it into the 3rd page. This object can be used as it is, in the 2nd page.

Could you please explain me why ? If this object is no more available in our used version (2026.02), what is replacing it, and how could I replace it correctly ?

entry.title: Integrate CAP application as outbound channel for SAP Document AI
entry.content:
The Challenge

When integrating SAP Document AI as a document extraction engine into a broader BTP solution, one of the first hurdles you’ll encounter is the outbound callback mechanism. Document AI supports notifying an external system once extraction is complete — a great feature for building event-driven, asynchronous document processing pipelines.

However, there’s a subtle but important quirk: Document AI unconditionally performs a CSRF token preflight before sending the actual extraction result callback. It fires a GET request with the header X-CSRF-Token: Fetch to your callback endpoint regardless of whether CSRF protection is actually in place on the receiving end.

This behavior cannot be disabled in the Document AI configuration.

The solution? Register Express middleware directly during CAP’s bootstrap phase — before the framework takes over — and handle both the preflight and the callback there.

Architecture Overview

SAP Document AI
      │
      │  ① GET /callback/notify
      │     X-CSRF-Token: Fetch
      │◄─── X-CSRF-Token: <random-token>
      │
      │  ② POST /callback/notify
      │     X-CSRF-Token: <random-token>
      │     Body: notification payload
      ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
│          CAP Application             │
│                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │     csrf-middleware.js         │  │
│  │  • Issues token on GET/HEAD    │  │
│  │  • Validates token on POST     │  │
│  └───────────────┬────────────────┘  │
│                  │                   │
│  ┌───────────────▼────────────────┐  │
│  │     callback-service.js        │  │
│  │  • Parses extraction payload   │  │
│  │  • Matches & finalises job     │  │
│  └────────────────────────────────┘  │
└──────────────────────────────────────┘

The middleware intercepts both requests before CAP’s own routing and CSRF logic runs, acting as a transparent proxy layer between Document AI and your business logic.

Implementation Walkthrough

1. Hooking into the CAP Bootstrap

The key to this entire approach is cds.on('bootstrap', (app) => { ... }). This event fires after CAP has initialized the underlying Express application but before it registers its own middleware stack. This gives us direct, unmediated access to the raw Express app object.

The server.js is deliberately thin. Its only responsibility is wiring the CSRF middleware into the Express stack at the right moment:

'use strict';

const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const csrfMiddleware = require('./csrf-middleware');

cds.on('bootstrap', (app) => {
    app.use(csrfMiddleware);
});

module.exports = cds.server;

⚠️Exporting cds.server is essential — this tells CAP to use the standard server startup while still respecting your bootstrap customizations.

The actual callback business logic lives separately in callback-service.js, keeping concerns cleanly separated. The middleware handles the HTTP-level CSRF handshake; the service handles what to do with the payload.

2. The CSRF Middleware

The full middleware lives in csrf-middleware.js and handles two responsibilities: issuing tokens on preflight requests and validating tokens on incoming POST requests.

Token Lifecycle

Rather than returning a static or meaningless string, the middleware generates a cryptographically random token per preflight, stores it in memory with a TTL, and enforces single-use consumption:

// In-memory token store: token → expiry timestamp
const _tokens = new Map();
const TOKEN_TTL_MS = 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 minutes

function generateToken() {
    const token = crypto.randomBytes(24).toString('hex');
    _tokens.set(token, Date.now() + TOKEN_TTL_MS);
    return token;
}

function validateToken(token) {
    if (!token) return false;
    const expiry = _tokens.get(token);
    if (!expiry) return false;
    if (Date.now() > expiry) { _tokens.delete(token); return false; }
    _tokens.delete(token); // single-use: consumed on first valid POST
    return true;
}

A periodic cleanup interval ensures expired tokens don’t accumulate indefinitely:

setInterval(() => {
    const now = Date.now();
    for (const [token, expiry] of _tokens.entries()) {
        if (expiry < now) _tokens.delete(token);
    }
}, 60000);

The Middleware Function

The middleware is path-scoped to /callback/* routes and handles each HTTP method appropriately:

function csrfMiddleware(req, res, next) {
    // Only apply to /callback paths
    if (!req.path.startsWith('/callback')) return next();

    const csrfHeader = req.headers['x-csrf-token'];

    // GET or HEAD preflight: issue a fresh token
    if ((req.method === 'GET' || req.method === 'HEAD') 
            && csrfHeader?.toLowerCase() === 'fetch') {
        const token = generateToken();
        log.debug(`CSRF preflight (${req.method}): issued token for ${req.path}`);
        res.set('X-CSRF-Token', token);
        res.set('Allow', 'GET,HEAD,POST');
        res.set('Cache-Control', 'no-cache, no-store');
        return res.status(200).end();
    }

    // POST: validate the token if present
    if (req.method === 'POST') {
        if (csrfHeader && !validateToken(csrfHeader)) {
            log.warn(`CSRF validation failed for POST ${req.path}`);
            return res.status(403).json({ 
                error: { message: 'CSRF token validation failed' } 
            });
        }
        log.debug(`CSRF token accepted for POST ${req.path}`);
    }

    next();
}

3. Handling the Callback Payload

Once the CSRF handshake is complete, Document AI posts the extraction results to /callback/notify. The actual processing logic is registered as a standard CAP service endpoint in callback-service.js.

What Document AI Sends

This is where it gets interesting. SAP Document AI schemas have a toggle that controls how much data is included directly in the callback payload. Depending on your schema configuration, you’ll encounter two patterns:

Option A — Full extraction result in the callback body

When the "include extraction result" toggle is enabled on your Document AI schema, the callback payload contains the complete extraction result directly in the DocumentResult field — serialized as a Python dictionary string representation (not valid JSON). This means you get all extracted field values, confidence scores, and metadata in a single push, with no need to make a follow-up API call to retrieve results.

DocumentResult = "{file_ID=abc-123, extracted_fields=[...], schema=InvoiceSchema, ...}"

⚠️ Note that this is Python repr() format, not JSON. Key-value pairs use = instead of :, and null values appear as None. Your callback handler needs to account for this — either with a dedicated parser or targeted regex extraction for the fields you need.

Option B — Lightweight notification only

When the toggle is disabled (or a simpler integration is preferred), the callback only contains a minimal notification with the NotificationObjectId. You can then use this ID to fetch the structured extraction result on demand via the Document AI DocumentService:

GET /document-service/v1/DocumentService/Documents({NotificationObjectId})/DocumentService.simpleStructure()

The DocumentService.simpleStructure() endpoint returns a clean, normalized JSON representation of the extracted fields — often the more practical choice if you don’t need the full raw extraction metadata and want a consistent, version-stable data contract between Document AI and your CAP application.

Setting Up the Callback Destination

With the CSRF middleware in place, the next step is making the callback URL reachable from SAP Document AI. This involves two things: defining the destination in your mta.yaml so it is automatically provisioned on deployment (ensuring automatic client credential updates), and then registering it as an outbound channel in the Document AI schema configuration UI.

Defining the Destination in mta.yaml

SAP BTP Destination Service allows you to declare destinations declaratively as part of your MTA deployment descriptor. This means the callback destination is created or updated automatically every time you deploy — no manual destination configuration in the BTP cockpit required.

Add a dedicated content deployer module to your mta.yaml:

# ─── Destination content deployer ─────────────────────────────────────────────
- name: docai-cap-dest-content
  type: com.sap.application.content
  requires:
    - name: docai-cap-dest # the name of your destination resource
      parameters:
        content-target: true
    - name: docai-cap-xsuaa # the name of your xsuaa resource
      parameters:
        service-key:
          name: docai-cap-xsuaa-key
    - name: srv-api
  parameters:
    content:
      subaccount:
        existing_destinations_policy: update
        destinations:
          - Name: CAP_DocAI_Callback
            Description: Callback destination for CAP app
            URL: ~{srv-url}/callback/notify
            Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
            TokenServiceInstanceName: docai-cap-xsuaa
            TokenServiceKeyName: docai-cap-xsuaa-key
            HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
  build-parameters:
    no-source: tru

A few things worth calling out:

~{srv-api/srv-url}/callback/notify
The URL is not hardcoded — it is resolved dynamically at deployment time using the srv-api provided parameters of your CAP server module. This means the destination always points to the correct URL regardless of which BTP space or region you deploy to, making the descriptor fully portable across landscapes.

OAuth2ClientCredentials
The callback destination is secured with OAuth2 Client Credentials, backed by the XSUAA instance of your CAP application. When Document AI calls the callback URL, it first fetches a Bearer token from XSUAA using the client credentials stored in docai-cap-scanner-xsuaa-key, and includes it in the Authorization header of every request — both the CSRF preflight GET and the actual callback POST. Your CAP application’s standard XSUAA-based authentication middleware validates this token automatically.

existing_destinations_policy: update
This ensures that redeployments update the destination in place rather than failing on a name conflict. Safe to use in CI/CD pipelines.

HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
Marks the destination as dynamically resolvable, which is required for runtime URL resolution via the destination service.

Configuring the Outbound Channel in SAP Document AI

Once deployed, the destination is available in your BTP subaccount and can be referenced directly from the SAP Document AI configuration UI. Navigate to Channels in Document AI and create an Outbound Channel ensuring the toggle for "Use SAP BTP Destination" is enabled.

 

Once saved, go to your schema, navigate to the Outbound Channel Configuration and add the just configured channel and toggle on/off the inclusion of your extraction results. Specifying the Document Status in which the channel shall be triggered allows you to either immediately forward the notification once the extraction is done (statusReview Needed) or only after a human validation (e.g. status Confirmed) .

With both the destination provisioned automatically via MTA and the outbound channel registered in the Document AI schema UI, the entire integration is reproducible from a single mbt build && cf deploy — no manual post-deployment steps required.

Conclusion

SAP Document AI is a powerful extraction service, but its fixed CSRF preflight behavior creates a real integration challenge when connecting to CAP applications or Integration Flows. By hooking into CAP’s bootstrap lifecycle and registering a focused, well-structured Express middleware, we can elegantly satisfy Document AI’s handshake requirements without patching CAP internals or weakening the application’s overall security posture.

The result is a clean two-layer architecture: the middleware owns the CSRF token lifecycle at the HTTP layer, and the callback service owns the business logic of matching jobs and processing extraction results — each with a single, clear responsibility.

This pattern is also broadly reusable: any time you need to expose a raw HTTP endpoint in a CAP application that bypasses the standard framework middleware (for webhooks, health checks, or third-party callbacks with unusual requirements), cds.on('bootstrap') is your entry point.

Happy building! 🚀

entry.title: Re: Compliance form did not trigger for external candidat...
entry.content: As per the backend logs, the error is caused due to stateNav not available in perAddressDEFLT Entity. You can simply go to Manage Business Configuration > homeAddress_GBR > State > Details > Type of reference object: picklist > Picklist: STATE_GBR. This should ensure that the stateNav is added to the entity. Post that you can test with any new candidate or restart an existing user.

entry.title: The Controller's Favourite: A Business Calculator Custom Widget for SAP Analytics Cloud Tables
entry.content:
The Problem Every Controller Knows

You are staring at a planning table in SAP Analytics Cloud. Actuals in one column, budget in another, forecast in a third. You need a quick deviation — not a new calculated measure, not a story-level formula, just a fast ad-hoc number to sanity-check what you are seeing.

What do you do? You reach for Excel. Or the Windows calculator. You mentally note the values, switch applications, type them in, get your number, and switch back.

This is all the past, with the SAC BusinessCalculator Custom Widget.

What the Widget Does

The Business Calculator Custom Widget offers:

Standard Mode covers everyday arithmetic: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, percentage, and sign toggle. Nothing surprising — it behaves like the calculator on your phone, with one meaningful difference: you do not need to type numbers manually.

 The SAC BusinessCalculator Custom Widget - Using single cell calculations

 The SAC BusinessCalculator Custom Widget - Using multi cell selection calculations

The Key Feature: Click-to-Load from SAC Table Cells

The interaction that makes this widget genuinely useful in a planning context is **cell-click integration**.

In a SAC Analytical Application, the Table Widget exposes an `onClick` event handler in the Script API. The Business Calculator registers two operand slots — **Slot A** and **Slot B**. The active slot (which one gets filled on the next click) is toggled directly inside the widget or can be set via a property from the AAp script.

The flow looks like this:

User clicks Actual cell in Table Widget
→ AAp Script reads event.value + event.memberPath
→ Script calls BusinessCalc.setProperty("slotAValue", cellValue)
→ Widget receives onCustomWidgetAfterUpdate({ slotAValue: "1250000" })
→ Slot A loads the value, display label updates to cell context
→ User clicks Plan cell
→ Slot B loads

No copy-paste. No context switch. The controller stays in SAC, stays in flow.

Multi-cell selection is also supported: when a user selects a range in the table, the widget accumulates the values and adds them — useful for summing a set of cost centre rows before comparing to a total budget line.

Architecture Overview

The widget follows the standard SAC Custom Widget SDK pattern: a single `widget.json` manifest and a single `widget.js` implementation file, packaged as a ZIP and uploaded via SAC Administration → Custom Widgets.

sac-business-calc/
├── widget.json ← Manifest: properties, events, metadata
├── widget.js ← Custom Element (Shadow DOM, no external deps)
└── test/
└── mock-sac.html ← Local SAC runtime simulator for development

Configurable Properties at a Glance

| Property | Type | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `defaultMode` | Dropdown | `standard` | Open in Standard or Business mode |
| `locale` | String | `de-DE` | Number formatting |
| `activeSlot` | Dropdown | `A` | Which slot fills on next cell click |
| `slotALabel` | String | `Slot A` | Display label for operand A |
| `slotBLabel` | String | `Slot B` | Display label for operand B |
| `slotAValue` | String | *(empty)* | Set by AAp script on cell click |
| `slotBValue` | String | *(empty)* | Set by AAp script on cell click |
| `writeBack` | Boolean | `false` | Fire `onResult` event with result payload |
| `highlightColor` | String | `#0070f2` | Accent colour for header and primary buttons |

 

AAp Script Integration (Minimal Example)

Connecting the widget to a Table Widget in your Analytical Application requires just a few lines in the SAC Script editor. Note the SAC Script constraints: single-argument `console.log`, no ternary operators, no `JSON.stringify`.

// Table_1 onClick handler in the AAp Script
Table_1.onClick = function(event) {
var cellValue = event.value;
var memberLabel = event.memberPath;
var slot = BusinessCalc_1.getProperties().activeSlot;

if (slot === "A") {
BusinessCalc_1.setProperty("slotAValue", cellValue);
BusinessCalc_1.setProperty("slotALabel", memberLabel);
} else {
BusinessCalc_1.setProperty("slotBValue", cellValue);
BusinessCalc_1.setProperty("slotBLabel", memberLabel);
}
};

For write-back scenarios, the `onResult` event can be wired to update a planning input control or trigger a data action — turning the calculator from a read-only analysis tool into part of a lightweight what-if workflow.

Where to Use It

A few scenarios where the widget adds immediate value in a real planning environment:

- Budget review meetings — quickly validate a deviation a stakeholder spots in a table without leaving the application
- Rolling forecast sessions — compare new FC to old FC across selected cells in seconds
- Cost centre deep dives — sum a selection of line items and compare to a subtotal in one motion
- Ad-hoc margin checks — enter revenue and cost from the table; Business Mode returns the margin instantly

Every day you work in SAC, the business Calculator can help you getting your needed calculation and checks done right in SAC.

This Is What SAC and BDC Extensibility Looks Like in Practice

One thing this project illustrates well: the extensibility of SAP Analytics Cloud — and of SAP Business Data Cloud as the broader platform — is not just a slide deck talking point.

The Custom Widget SDK is deliberately open. Your widget is a Web Component. The interface contract is small: a manifest, a lifecycle hook, and an event. Everything between those boundaries is yours. You bring your own logic, your own UX, your own domain knowledge. SAC handles the embedding, the property binding, the runtime, and the integration with live planning data.

That openness matters more than it might seem. Controllers and FP&A teams have calculation needs that are highly specific — not generic enough to become a product feature, but recurring enough to be genuinely painful. A business calculator with variance analysis and forecast-delta built in is not something SAP will ever ship as a standard widget. It does not need to. The extensibility model exists precisely for this kind of domain-specific micro-tool.

The same principle applies at the platform level with BDC. The combination of governed, semantically rich data from Datasphere, live planning writeback via SAC, and an open widget layer means that domain experts — not just developers — can extend the user experience to match how their finance organisation actually works. The barrier is low enough that a use case like this one can go from idea to deployed widget in a weekend, with a bit of vibe coding and the right AI assistant alongside.

The message for any SAC or BDC customer: you do not have to wait for the roadmap. If you can describe what your controllers need, you can build it.

Summary

The Business Calculator Custom Widget is a small addition to a SAC Analytical Application that eliminates a daily friction point for controllers and FP&A professionals. Click a cell, click another cell, get your deviation. Stay in SAC.

It also demonstrates something more general: SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Data Cloud are platforms, not just products. The extensibility is real, the barrier is low, and the people who know best what controllers actually need — the consultants, the power users, the pre-sales folks who spend their days in planning sessions — are exactly the right people to build these kinds of micro-tools. A bit of domain knowledge, a Custom Widget manifest, and an AI coding assistant is genuinely all it takes.

The widget is available as a Custom Widget ZIP. Drop a comment below if you would like the source or have questions about the integration.

Let me know if you enjoy this kind of content — and if you have your own widget ideas or extensibility scenarios rattling around, drop them in the comments. I am curious what you are building.

Note: This is a personal use case and proof of concept, not an official SAP product announcement or recommendation. All views are the author's own.

entry.title: Romanian DSPL 394 – XML Errors
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working with the Romanian report “Statement 394 (DSPL)” and I am getting this error in the XML file, which I haven’t been able to fix:

<op1 tip="AI" tip_partener="2" ... />

It seems that SAP is marking the partner as tip_partener = 2, and ANAF rejects operations A and AI. Therefore, this is invalid for ANAF.

And another error with this block with this code:

<rezumat1 tip_partener="2" cota="21" facturiA="1" facturiAI="1" ... />

If tip_partener = 2, there cannot be facturiA or facturiAI.

From what I have checked, the issue seems to be in the Business Partner master data, because SAP continues classifying the Business Partner as a natural person.

I have reviewed the Business Partner and it has:

  • Country = RO
  • BP is marked as Organization
  • The tax identification seems correct: RO13279412

    Could you please help me?

    I am attaching the XML of the Romanian 394 report generated by the system.

    Thank you!

entry.title: Two days of SAP community in Weinheim: CodeJam and Inside Track, 18-19 September 2026
entry.content:
Block your September calendar, we've got something for you. Two free community events back to back in Weinheim: a SAP CodeJam on Friday the 18th, the first SAP InsideTrack Weinheim on Saturday the 19th, and a relaxed get-together on Friday evening for anyone already in town.

Everything lives on sitwhm.de. Registration is open for both, and the Call for Speakers is open too.



Friday, September 18: SAP CodeJam with DJ Adams

DJ Adams (@qmacro) is doing a full-day CodeJam on CAP CDS modelling. The aim is to walk out actually understanding how CDS works, rather than the usual "I know the word but couldn't explain it under pressure" situation.

Here's the thing: even if CAP isn't your daily driver, a CodeJam with DJ is a great day out. Small group (up to 50 people), laptops open, everyone working through the same exercises together, DJ roaming the room to help when things go sideways. You get to ask the question you've been too embarrassed to ask in your team chat, and the person next to you probably has the same one. By lunch you've learned something new, gotten unstuck on two things you didn't realise you were stuck on, and made a couple of new friends in the room - a selfie with DJ can't hurt either. 😉 By 4 PM you're tired in the good way.

If you already work with CAP day to day, you'll close some real gaps. If you don't, you'll leave with enough foundation to try it on a side project, plus a much better feel for how modern SAP development hangs together.

Date: Friday, September 18, 2026
Time: 9:00 to 16:00
Location: Syntax Auditorium, Weinheim
Host: DJ Adams
Topic: CAP CDS modelling
Capacity: 50 seats
Event information: Hands on with CAP CDS
Registration: ti.to/sitwhm/codejam-whm

Quick heads up: the CodeJam has its own signup, separate from the InsideTrack. If you want to do both, register for both.

Personal note: I hosted CodeJams (in my time at SAP back in the days) myself for a while - together with DJ and on my own, and I loved every single one of them. It really didn't matter whether the participants were seasoned SAP developers or total beginners, they were fun for everyone in the room. The format just works.


Friday evening: Pre-event get-together

From 7 PM on Friday we're meeting up somewhere in Weinheim for food and drinks. Casual, no agenda, just people in town for the weekend hanging out. Speakers, organisers, early arrivals, whoever's around. Come join us. Food and drinks are on your own. The venue will go up on sitwhm.de closer to the date - once we have an indication of how many of you would join. 😉 There's a question on the ticket registration for SIT to indicate your interest, so please let us know!

Saturday, September 19: SAP InsideTrack Weinheim

For anyone who hasn't been to a SIT: it's a free, community-organised event with short talks (20 to 40 minutes), proper breaks, and no commercial agenda. Honestly, the best bits are often the conversations in between the sessions, and that's kind of the point.

The last SAP InsideTrack in the Rhine-Neckar region was in Walldorf, quite a few years back now. We've been missing that kind of day in the region, so we're making one happen. And we'd really like you to be there. 

Date: Saturday, September 19, 2026
Time: 9:00 to 18:00
Location: Syntax Auditorium, Höhnerweg 2-4, 69469 Weinheim
Capacity: 150 seats
Cost: Free
Registration: ti.to/sitwhm/sitwhm2026

The venue is a five minute walk from Weinheim train station, has free parking on site, and is wheelchair accessible. If you have specific accessibility requirements, drop us a line at team@sitwhm.de so we can set things up for you.

Why both on consecutive days

Pretty simple. If you're travelling in, a weekend is easier to justify than a single day. And the two formats do different things: the CodeJam is hands-on and focused on one topic, the InsideTrack is about hearing a bunch of different things and catching up with people. They go well together, but you're very welcome to come for just one of them too.

Call for Speakers
Got something to share? Please send it in on sessionize.com/sitwhm2026
Pretty much any SAP topic works. AI and Joule, BTP, ABAP and ABAP Cloud, CAP, UI5 and Fiori, integration, analytics, cloud architecture, clean core, migrations, security, developer experience, side projects, community stuff. If you're into it, chances are others will be too.

First-time speakers, we'd love to have you. Plenty of SIT regulars gave their first ever talk at a SIT. If you're not sure whether your topic fits, submit it anyway and we can talk it through.

Session length: 20 or 40 minutes
Language: English only
No product pitches

Registration and links:

CodeJam on Friday, September 18: ti.to/sitwhm/codejam-whm
InsideTrack on Saturday, September 19: ti.to/sitwhm/sitwhm2026
Call for Speakers: sessionize.com/sitwhm2026
Website: sitwhm.de
Questions: team@sitwhm.de

It really helps us if you register early, even if September still feels far away. It makes catering and space planning so much easier on our end.

Sponsors and helping out
We're still looking for sponsors, mostly for catering and a few practical things around the day. If that's something your company might be open to, get in touch at team@sitwhm.de or via LinkedIn. Same goes if you want to help on the day itself.

The organising team is Katharina Roeder, Julian Breitkopf, Dr. Dominik Schaefer, @matt_steiner and myself. Big thanks to Syntax for hosting us at the auditorium on both days.

Again: We'd really love to see you in Weinheim in September. Whether you come for one day, both days, or just the Friday evening catch-up, you're very welcome. And if you post about it, tag #sitWHM so we can follow along.

entry.title: Re: Change log for reservation under lineitem
entry.content: In standard SAP, reservations do not have the same Environment tab change log as purchase orders. However, changes are recorded in CDHDR/CDPOS if the reservation change document object is active. To achieve visibility, you can either build a custom report to display reservation changes (who changed what, when), or enhance MB22 with a custom tab that calls the change log. This way, you will be able to track header and item changes for reservations just like you do in ME22N.

entry.title: IAS App-to-App Navigation Failing: UI5 AppRouter → CAP Node.js via Destination
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I’m trying to implement SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS / AMS) based authentication between two separately deployed BTP applications (MTAs), but the token exchange from UI5 AppRouter to CAP backend keeps failing.

I’d appreciate guidance on whether my architecture is valid, and what configuration I may have missed.

Landscape

Application A (Consumer)

UI5 frontend with standalone AppRouter

  • Deployed in BTP Cloud Foundry

  • Uses IAS login

  • Calls backend via Destination Service

Application B (Provider)

CAP Node.js service

  • Separate MTA

  • Protected by IAS / AMS

  • Has provided-apis configured

Expected Flow:

User
→ UI5 AppRouter
→ Destination (IASDependencyName)
→ AppRouter exchanges token
→ CAP backend receives exchanged token
→ CAP authorizes user

Current Destination Setup

Name: CAP_AMS_BACKEND
Type: HTTP
Authentication: NoAuthentication
URL: https://<cap-service-url>

Additional Properties:
HTML5.IASDependencyName = cap-ams //setup in IAS

I have also tried with destination type = oAuth2UserTokenExchange but got the same 401 error. Error screenshot below. Looks like the destination service client Id is the caller to IAS for token exchange but IAS is rejecting the audience.

UI5 App xs-app.json

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/$1",
      "destination": "CAP_AMS_BACKEND",
      "authenticationType": "ias"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "localDir": "resources",
      "authenticationType": "ias"
    }
  ]
}

 CAP App ias instance

UI5 App ias instance

 

Dependency set on Consumer --> Provider App in IAS

All the grant types are selected in the provider and the consumer app for testing

 
What I Want to Confirm:
  1. Is this architecture officially supported? Standalone UI5 AppRouter (MTA A) calling separate CAP Node.js app (MTA B) via destination using IAS App-to-App navigation?
  2. Should this work using only:
    Authentication = NoAuthentication / oAuth2UserTokenExchange
    HTML5.IASDependencyName = <dependencyName>
  3. Should both apps share the same IAS instance, or is separate IAS instance + dependency the correct model?
  4. Has anyone implemented this successfully with CAP Node.js?

Thanks in advance,

Deepak

entry.title: Re: FIORI app F2705 - how to add custom start conditions
entry.content:
Hi 

My name is Lisa from the MM-PUR team. I assume your need is to change to flexible workflow from release strategy approval. 

In this situation, to add custom start conditions to the flexible workflow we offer a BADI SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF, here you define the conditions and then they are available for selection within the Manage Workflows app. 

Then there is the counterpart BADI SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL, and here you code to evaluate the custom condition against the value in the document. 

For more information on these BADIs please check the SAP Help here: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/91ab576eaf58475f8934d2dcef16edb3.html?locale=en-US&version=2023.latest

Then select the option 'Optional: Custom Logic'. 

I hope this helps further, 

Kind Regards, 
Lisa 
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Important Update: Review Onboarding User Permissions
entry.content:
@Damian_Kolanek , @smitajalit @JulieMcSwain 

This is what I have found - During release, changes may require new permissions for certain features. The backend technical account user automatically updates the permissions, adding or removing them as needed, so that customers do not need to perform any manual configuration.

Therefore, the removal of the EC API permissions in the 1H release is an automated adjustment performed by the system. No manual re‑addition is required, and the necessary permissions will be applied automatically to ensure continued access for the technical team and other users.

- I am still testing however, thus far, I have not found any issues. 
-If anyone encounters issues to this change, please share!!! 🙂

entry.title: Re: Data persistence in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello,

this persistence problem happened to me several times. It´s usually caused by the wrong use of join cardinalities that can multiply rows.

Join cardinality settings are used for optimization and can lead to unexpected behaviors if the data doesn’t match the stated cardinality

Hope it helps!

entry.title: Re: Does PRIMARY KEY clustered check stored somewhere
entry.content: If you want to get the index name of the primary key regardless of whether it is clustered, remove the clustered_index_id predicate. Its only purpose was to limit the result set to clustered primary keys.

entry.title: Re: How to Capture the IOT version inside a Transport req...
entry.content:
https://me.sap.com/notes/3523704/E
In Standard its made like that to not capture the IOT in Transport. 

The reason is that the content of /SCWM/QIOT and  /SCWM/QIOTPRP tables are not copied with client copy due to its delivery class “A” - Application table (master and transaction data), while content of QIE_IOBTYP is copied to the target system.

The need to manually generate Inspection Object Type (IOT) versions in each client arises from the way client copies and data management are structured within the SAP system. Here are the key reasons:
1. Client-Specific Customization: Each client in the SAP environment can have its own set of configurations and customizations. When you perform a client copy, certain tables, particularly those related to IOTs, may not be copied due to their delivery class. For instance, the content of the /SCWM/QIOT and /SCWM/QIOTPRP tables, which are crucial for IOT versions, are not transferred during a client copy.
2. Version Activation: After a client copy, the system may not have the necessary IOT versions activated. This can lead to errors when trying to perform quality management processes, as the system relies on the correct IOT version being available and activated.
3. Error Prevention: Generating IOT versions manually helps prevent errors such as "No inspection object versions were activated" or issues related to incorrect IOT processing. By ensuring that the correct versions are generated and activated, you can maintain the integrity of quality management processes.
4. Consistency Across Clients: Manually generating IOT versions ensures that all clients have the same versions available, which is essential for consistent operations and reporting across different environments.
5. Control Over Customization: Manual generation allows for better control over the customization process, enabling administrators to verify and adjust settings as needed before activating new versions. 💡 Pro Tip(s):

  • Always implement relevant SAP notes, such as note 3523704, before generating new IOT versions, especially after a client copy.
  • Ensure there are no open or pending quality inspection documents before generating a new IOT version to avoid conflicts.

Would you like to know more about the specific steps for generating IOT versions? Your feedback on this response would be greatly appreciated!
To Solve that we need to activate the TR saving in SE54 for the tables /SCWM/QIOT and  /SCWM/QIOTPRP, /SCWM/V_QIOT


At begining if i display it was not there. So I genereated the object

entry.title: Re: Gagan Mohan Singh
entry.content:
Hi Lakshmitriveni, I am glad you reached out!

Given where SAP is headed, I'd say write about AI in MM. It's the most relevant thing right now and honestly, not enough practitioners are writing about it from a process angle.

Think about what's already changing — invoice processing, PO creation, vendor evaluation, GR matching. These are tasks that MM folks do every day, and AI is starting to take a lot of that off the plate. What does that mean for the user? Less firefighting, fewer errors, more time to actually think. And for the organisation? Leaner procurement without hiring more people.

You have hands-on P2P experience, so you can write about this in a way that's grounded — not just theoretical. That's what the community needs more of.

Go for it!

entry.title: Re: Unused Budget per Planner from Previous Compensation...
entry.content:
The detailed rollup report should give you this information.   The trick will be how to assign that budget to the manager who has the additional money.

This will depend on you budget type and if you use a bottom up approach where the budget contribution is assigned at the employee level and then aggregated for the manager, that money needs to be assigned to an employee record, assigning it to the manager places the budget one level higher.

You can assign to the manager (planner) if you cascade the budget, top down but that is a lot of administrative work and not commonly used.

entry.title: Re: Data persistence in Datasphere
entry.content: @Martin_Kuma I found the issue. It appears that there are 2 views that are left joined to the source view but no values match the source view. entry.title: Why ADSSAP is in lower SP29 with NW 7.5 SP32 for Solution Manager 7.2 SP21 Alone?
entry.content:
Solution Manager 7.2 SP21 released with NW 7.5 SP32 combo.
By default,  For each NW SP <-> Corresponding ADS SP will be there (same SP version)

Also, All the previous solution manager version will have same NW 7.5 SP and ADS SP version.
However, For the first time in history, SAP released with different levels such as NW 7.5 SP32 but ADSSAP still resides in SP29 which was the combo in older SP20 (N-1)



Usually, ADSSAP SP version will be released together with NW Release only. Unfortunately, I could see just one month delay for ADSSAP while NW Version 7.5 SP32 released.

Just curious?
I wonder, Why SAP didn't wait for one more month and release with perfect compatibility like they did for all older 7.5 SPs till SP20 ?
I wonder, When Standalone NW 7.5 SP32 still have NW 7.5 SP32, Why not Solution Manager 7.2 SP21 + NW 7.5 SP32 + ADSSAP SP32

In normal Standalone cases such as Netweaver Java Enterprise Portal and others, We will definitely have same SP version only. If NW 7.5 SP31, ADSSAP would be SP31 only. Maintenance planner choses the same (except this solution manager case). Won't be possible to update higher/downgrade SP version as well.

It's still mystery for me that this deviates years of release behavior and suddenly, we have a release with combo (fine compatibility with different ADS version).

Posting this to get your basis experiences as well.  I will add the same in my tips documentation/site (link in my profile - 15th - https://profile.sap.com/u/AJAYTR) for reference. It would be helpful to other basis peoples if someone got curious on the same like me.

Thanks.

NW ABAP Business Rule Framework (BRFplus) NW ABAP Print and Output Management

entry.title: Re: New POD 2.0 Framework: Dynamic PODs, How to Dynamically Show, Hide, Enable, and Disable UI Eleme
entry.content:
Hello @ManoelCosta ,
thanks for the great plog post!
Is there any further information about possible Syntax? I assume this should be regular expression binding as documented here Expression Binding - Documentation - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK?
Could you give us a hint on how we could use this to e.g. make buttons visible if a POD variable is not null/contains a value?
In our use case (I guess this will be interesting for many customers), we only want to show the majority of the buttons whenever an operator is labored on. So we created a production process which is returning the string of users currently labored on on the selected SFC (in the beta this was in the POD context, now not anymore). This is then stored as POD variable. Whenever this variable is not existing or empty, we want to hide certain buttons.
We already tried the following syntax without success:

  • {= %{/listOfAssignedOperators/outString} !== ''}
  • {= %{/listOfAssignedOperators/outString} !== ""}
  • {= %{/listOfAssignedOperators/outString} !== null }
  • {= typeof %{/listOfAssignedOperators/outString} !== 'undefined'}

We would highly appreciate your input!
Thanks a lot!
Simon

entry.title: Re: Variance calculation without Target cost
entry.content: The system shows resource usage and output price variances because it derives target costs from the order’s planned activities and planned output, not only from the material’s standard cost estimate. Even without a standard cost, the order itself provides planned values that allow the system to calculate these variance categories. The warning message simply indicates that no material cost estimate exists, so the system cannot calculate target costs at the material level; but order‑level plan/actual comparisons are still possible.

entry.title: Re: Error: FBV0: No batch input data found for dynpro SAP...
entry.content: The failure in FBV0 happens because the SCB Indicator is reset during parking and not re‑derived at posting. While batch input posting can be used as a temporary workaround, the recommended long‑term solution is to implement an enhancement (your option 3) that automatically populates the SCB Indicator during posting. This ensures cross‑company postings remain automated and compliant with country‑specific requirements

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud App Create Advance Return on Sales/Purch...
entry.content:
Below are the key considerations

1) Country Template
Ensure NL template is activated in configuration (SSCUI for tax reporting).

2) Master Data
VAT registration number and BP address must be maintained correctly for NL company codes.

3) Compliance
The XML file format is aligned with Dutch tax authority specifications.

4) Repeat Runs
If corrections are needed, rerun the app with adjusted parameters and regenerate the file.

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
As you reference SP03 and that the install gets stuck, it is very likely you are hitting a known issue. SP03 is an older code line and I assume you may be using an older PL level as well. Does the ASE startup log show signal 11 error with no function in Address:

Current process (0x1d000f) infected with signal 11 (SIGSEGV)
Address 0x0x00007efda8089360 () ...

followed by a stack trace that includes the function Snap::Validate:

()
Snap::Validate
SnapValidation
dsinit

followed by
Warning: The internal timer is not progressing. If this message is generated multiple times, report to SAP Technical Support and restart the server (alarminterval=0).


If so,  you are hitting an issue because of an incompatibility between ASE compiler and Linux OS (patch) versions. You need to use at least :
- ASE 16.0 SP03 PL11 or
- ASE 16.0 SP04 PL01

 If you are starting out again, I would not recommend to go with SP03 but install latest, which is SP04 PL08.

Kind regards

Bart
